title: 8 Herbal Creams For Colds
intro_desc: Herbal creams for colds are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms and speed up recovery. They work by using the antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of certain herbs to target the root causes of the cold. For example, Eucalyptus globulus, also known as eucalyptus, is commonly used in herbal creams because of its decongestant properties. When applied topically, eucalyptus helps to relieve congestion and coughs, making it easier to breathe.  Another herb that is often used in herbal creams for colds is Echinacea purpurea, also known as coneflower. Echinacea has immune-boosting properties that help to fight off the underlying infection that is causing the cold. By using an Echinacea-based cream, you can help to support your body's natural defenses and reduce the severity of your symptoms.  Ginger, or Zingiber officinale, is also a popular herb used in herbal creams for colds. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat and sinuses. When applied topically, ginger can also help to relieve coughs and sore throats.  Using herbal creams for colds can bring many benefits to your life. For one, they are a natural and chemical-free alternative to over-the-counter medications, which can have harsh side effects. Additionally, herbal creams can be used in conjunction with other natural remedies, such as steam inhalation and rest, to create a comprehensive treatment plan. By using herbal creams, you can take a proactive approach to managing your cold symptoms and reducing your reliance on pharmaceuticals.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains the active constituents eucalyptol and alpha-pinene, which have decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to relieve congestion and reduce the swelling of the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe. Eucalyptol also has antiviral properties, which can help to combat the underlying viral infection causing the cold. The anti-inflammatory properties of alpha-pinene can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce discomfort. By reducing congestion and inflammation, eucalyptus globulus creams can help to alleviate the symptoms of a cold.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1/2 cup of coconut oil.
Steep the dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves in the coconut oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the leaves.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil mixture, melting them in a double boiler.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool, then use the Eucalyptus globulus cream as needed for cold relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids that help with colds. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease congestion in the nasal passages. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells that fight off infections. The glycosides in Echinacea purpurea also have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the underlying bacterial or viral causes of a cold. By addressing both the symptoms and the underlying causes of a cold, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide relief from cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted oil mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained oil mixture with vitamin E oil and shea butter in a bowl and mix well until smooth.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use. Label and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like zingerone and gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe sore throats and calm congestion. The compounds in ginger also have natural decongestant properties that help break down mucus and open airways, making it easier to breathe. The antiviral properties of gingerol also help fight off the underlying viral infections that cause colds. Additionally, ginger has natural pain-relieving properties that can help ease headaches and body aches associated with colds. By applying Zingiber officinale creams topically, you can experience these benefits and get relief from cold symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale powder, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until fully melted.
In a separate bowl, mix Zingiber officinale powder with vitamin E oil until well combined.
Add the Zingiber officinale mixture to the melted coconut oil mixture and stir until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar, add peppermint essential oil, and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol, carvacrol, and other active constituents that help fight colds. Thymol is a natural antiseptic that kills bacteria and viruses, while carvacrol has decongestant properties that relieve sinus congestion and coughs. The thymol in Thymus vulgaris creams also has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the throat and nose. Additionally, the cream's antimicrobial properties help to prevent the spread of infection, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. By using Thymus vulgaris creams, you can help to alleviate cold symptoms and recover faster.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the dried Thymus vulgaris leaves. Let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl and let it cool. Discard the leaves.
In a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil or shea butter with 2 tablespoons of beeswax. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the cooled Thymus vulgaris liquid with the melted coconut oil mixture. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid and camphor, which are known for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat the underlying causes of a cold, such as a bacterial or viral infection. The camphor in Salvia officinalis creams also has a decongestant effect, which can help to relieve sinus pressure and congestion. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of rosmarinic acid can help to soothe a sore throat and reduce the severity of cold symptoms. By using Salvia officinalis creams, you can help to support your body's natural defenses and promote a faster recovery from a cold.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours, or until the mixture cools down.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Add the vitamin E oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe when you have a cold. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the throat, which can help to ease a sore throat and cough. The flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams have antioxidant properties that can help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can exacerbate cold symptoms. By reducing inflammation and soothing the throat, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from cold symptoms and help you feel better.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture, and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in the vitamin E oil, and pour the mixture into a clean container. Let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and terpinen-4-ol. These bioactive constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat cold symptoms. Cineole and limonene work together to reduce congestion and ease coughs, while terpinen-4-ol's anti-inflammatory effects soothe sore throats and ease breathing difficulties. The antimicrobial properties of these compounds also help prevent the spread of infections, making it easier to recover from a cold. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with colds.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once the mixture is melted, remove it from the heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Add the distilled water and Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream for colds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils with bioactive constituents like camphor, borneol, and borneol acetate. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help fight off infections and reduce congestion. The camphor in Rosmarinus officinalis creams has a decongestant effect, relieving nasal congestion and making it easier to breathe. Borneol and borneol acetate have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams topically, you can help ease cold symptoms and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for colds
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Also, get 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Steep the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in 2 tablespoons of olive oil for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Combine the melted oil mixture, the infused Rosmarinus officinalis oil, and 2 drops of your preferred essential oil (if using) in a bowl. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream for colds is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help boost your immune system and reduce the risk of catching a cold. Some herbal teas, like echinacea and peppermint, have properties that can help fight off germs and soothe a sore throat. Staying hydrated and getting enough rest also plays a role in preventing colds from forming.
faq_safe: You can consume herbal teas for colds every day, but in moderation. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, can be safe to drink in large quantities. However, teas like echinacea and sage may cause stomach problems if taken daily. It's best to listen to your body and adjust the amount you drink based on how you feel.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help soothe a cold, but it's not a magic fix. Some people notice relief within a few hours, while others might feel better after a day or two. This can vary depending on the tea and the individual. As your body fights off the infection, herbal teas can support you with their natural properties.
faq_time: When you have a cold, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning. This helps loosen up mucus and makes it easier to breathe. Your body is also more active in the morning, so the tea can help boost your immune system and get you feeling better faster.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for colds. Some herbal teas contain ingredients that can affect how your body processes certain medications. For example, ginseng and feverfew may increase the risk of bleeding when taken with blood-thinning medications. It's also possible that herbal teas can change the effectiveness of prescription medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas for colds. Some teas can affect the baby's growth or cause complications. For example, peppermint, ginger, and chamomile are usually safe in small amounts, but it's best to check the ingredients and follow the recommended dosages.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for colds can be a soothing relief when you're breastfeeding. However, it's essential to be cautious with what you drink. Some herbs, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe. Others, like sage and dandelion, can slow down milk production or interact with medications. Just be sure to choose teas that are specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding.
title_2: Teaching with a Cold? Try These 8 Herbal Creams
